delivery.raw: 主に対面（全開講回数の過半数）、一部オンライン

summary: We'll be looking at doctor/patient conversations regarding various medical conditions, the symptoms of those conditions, and medical vocabulary related to medical professionals, anatomy, common illnesses, and body systems.
aims:
This course will encourage first year students to improve their reading, writing, discussion and analytic skills in English though using medically based materials in English. Students will read about and discuss topics related to medicine and the sciences using a textbook and selections from journal articles or essays dealing with current issues in science or medicine.
Students will practice speaking, listening, reading, and writing skills in English, and will be expected to actively participate in class activities. The topic based course is aimed at developing students: analytical thinking skills and helping to create a greater awareness and understanding of current medical topics and issues.
goals: Students should be able to talk about hospital jobs, places in the hospital, responsibilites of hospital personnel
to describe first aid procedures and symptoms of illness
to be able to talk about their work and/or training as nurses; interviewing skills

plan.text: Greetings and Introduction/Medical Personnel/Introducing Medical Terminology
Chapter 1 - Fever/Medical Interview - chief complaint and diagnosis
Chapter 2 - Anemia - giving a blood test. Vocabulary test on 'further study' words.
Chapter 3 - Dehydration - loss of appetite. Vocabulary test on 'further study' words.
Chapter 4 - Obesity - diet, BMI, and eating related disorders.
Chapter 5 - Headache, describing aches and pains. Doctor's talk and patient talk.
Chapter 6 - Medical Interview on chest pain. Heart related conditions. Body systems. Vocabulary test on words in Chapter 5.
Special guest lecturer on heart attack and stroke.
Chapter 7 - Cough and the Respiratory System. Vocabulary test on 'further study' words in Chapter 6.
Chapter 8 - Abdominal Pain; the digestive system. Vocabulary test on 'further study' words in Chapter 7.
Chapter 9 - Dysphagia. Explanation of medical suffixes. Vocabulary test on 'further study' words in Chapter 8.
Chapter 10 - Hearing Loss, ENT disease. Vocabulary test on words from Chapter 9.
Chapter 12 - Pregnancy. Possible student pair presentations.
Final Examination. Possible short papers on medical conditions.

textbooks:
Students must buy the textbook Live Well! Topics About Health for University Students, by Adam Murray.
Other material from English for Medical Purposes, Step 1, and The Medical Book by Clifford Pickover will be supplied by the instructor.
prereq: This is a required course for first year medical students.
prep: Students will be required to do reading assignments in the textbook and study for the weekly medical vocabulary quizzes.

officeHour.name: Daniel B. Ribble
officeHour.place: The 2nd floor of Kochi Medical Departments General Classroom building.

extra.label: 成績評価に関する補足
extra.text:
The following factors will be taken into account in determining student scores.
1. Class attendance. Students must attend at least 2/3 of the classes to get a passing grade. Students over 20 minutes late will be considered absent. Two late attendances will equal one absence in this class.
2. Online assignments (20%)
3. Medical vocabulary quizzes (30%)
4. Examinations (50%)
Students must have a final score of 60 to pass the course.

extra.label: 教員の実務経験の有無
